heading: Transfer from Capitol Guide Service
label: 2252 Transfer from Capitol Guide Service
notes: References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in subsec. (a), was in the original “this title”, meaning title IV of , , , which is classified principally to this subchapter. For complete classification of title IV to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 110–437122 Stat. 4990Oct. 20, 2008

children.heading: Transfer of authorities and personnel of Congressional Special Services Office of Capitol Guide Service
children.label: a Transfer of authorities and personnel of Congressional Special Services Office of Capitol Guide Service

children.text: the contracts, liabilities, records, property, appropriations, and other assets and interests of the Congressional Special Services Office of the Capitol Guide Service, and the employees of such Office, are transferred to the Office of Congressional Accessibility Services established under (as amended by ), except that the transfer of any amounts appropriated to the Congressional Special Services Office that remain available as of the transfer date shall occur only upon the approval of the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and Senate; andsection 2172(a) of this titlesection 2251 of this title
the employees of such Office shall be subject to the direction, supervision, and control of the Director of Accessibility Services.

children.heading: Treatment of employees at time of transfer
children.label: b Treatment of employees at time of transfer

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any individual who is an employee of the Congressional Special Services Office of the Capitol Guide Service on a non-temporary basis on the transfer date who is transferred under subsection (a) shall be subject to the authority of the Director of Accessibility Services under (as amended by ), except that the individual’s grade, compensation, rate of leave, or other benefits that apply with respect to the individual at the time of transfer shall not be reduced while such individual remains continuously so employed in the same position within the Office of Congressional Accessibility Services established under (as amended by ), other than for cause.section 2172(b) of this titlesection 2251 of this titlesection 2172(a) of this titlesection 2251 of this title

children.heading: Eligibility for immediate retirement on basis of involuntary separation
children.label: 2 Eligibility for immediate retirement on basis of involuntary separation
A
children.text: the individual has completed 25 years of service under such title; or

children.label: B
children.text: the individual has completed 20 years of service under such title and is 50 years of age or older.

children.heading: Prohibiting imposition of probationary period
children.label: 3 Prohibiting imposition of probationary period
children.text: The Director of Accessibility Services may not impose a period of probation with respect to the transfer of any individual who is transferred to the Office of Congressional Accessibility Services under subsection (a).
